original_abstract: Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) represents a major global health challenge, characterized by progressive airway obstruction and significant systemic effects that contribute to high morbidity and mortality. While the clinical focus is often on respiratory function, comorbid mental health disorders—specifically depression and anxiety—frequently coexist with COPD, affecting approximately half of the patient population. These psychological factors are often overlooked in clinical settings despite their profound impact on patient quality of life and their tendency to exacerbate the physical symptoms of the disease. This review examines the high prevalence of depressive symptoms among COPD patients and highlights the necessity of integrating psychological assessment into routine care. Furthermore, the paper provides a comprehensive overview of the various diagnostic tools and standardized questionnaires currently utilized to screen for and evaluate the severity of depression in this vulnerable population.
humanized_abstract: Background Chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD) is a critical global public health issue characterized by airflow limitation and associated with numerous systemic complications and high levels of morbidity and mortality. Despite the fact that the symptoms of COPD manifest themselves in the lung, psychiatric co-morbidities such as depression and anxiety are common in the patients with COPD. It is estimated that more than half of the patients with COPD have depression and/or anxiety; yet, mental health issues are not always identified in the patients with COPD because they are not sufficiently explored in the clinical settings and because the symptoms of psychiatric morbidity can be disguised or masked by their primary respiratory condition. Therefore, this review is aimed at reviewing the high prevalence of depressive symptoms in the patients with COPD and the importance of including the psychiatric assessment in the management strategies of the patients with COPD, in addition to reviewing the available assessment tools for the diagnosis and evaluating the severity of depressive disorders in COPD patients.
